new_tasks.name: priority-reorder-api-and-ui
new_tasks.description: Add PUT endpoint for updating priority order, add reorder endpoints (move up/down), and implement up/down buttons in frontend priorities page.
new_tasks.instruction:
BACKEND:
1. Add PUT /api/priorities/{priority_id} endpoint to update priority_order of existing priority. Require purchase:execute. Log audit UPDATE.
2. Add POST /api/priorities/reorder endpoint accepting {country, items: [{id, priority_order}]} for batch reorder. Atomic Firestore batch write. Require purchase:execute.

FRONTEND (priorities/page.tsx):
3. Add Up/Down arrow buttons in table actions column.
4. On click Up: swap priority_order with item above, call reorder API.
5. On click Down: swap with item below.
6. Disable Up on first row, Down on last row.
7. Add success/error feedback on reorder.

TESTS:
8. Unit test for PUT endpoint.
9. Unit test for reorder endpoint.
10. Test edge cases: reorder single item, reorder non-existent ID.

acceptance_criteria.text: PUT /api/priorities/{id} updates priority_order and logs audit UPDATE
POST /api/priorities/reorder atomically reorders multiple priorities
Frontend shows Up/Down buttons that swap priority_order values
Up disabled on first row, Down disabled on last row

new_tasks.name: priority-lock-during-autobuy
new_tasks.description: Lock priority UI and API during active auto-buy. Check buy_locks collection before allowing priority changes.
new_tasks.instruction:
BACKEND (priorities/router.py):
1. In POST and DELETE endpoints, before writing: query buy_locks collection for active lock on requested country.
2. If lock exists and not expired: return HTTP 409 Conflict with message 'Auto-buy in progress for {country}. Priority changes locked.'
3. Add GET /api/priorities/lock-status?country=XX endpoint returning {locked: bool, locked_since: timestamp|null}.

FRONTEND (priorities/page.tsx):
4. Query lock-status on page load and on interval (every 10s).
5. When locked: disable Add Priority button, disable Delete buttons, disable Up/Down buttons, show Alert banner 'Priorities locked - auto-buy in progress for {country}'.
6. When unlocked: normal behavior.

TESTS:
7. Test POST returns 409 when lock active.
8. Test DELETE returns 409 when lock active.
9. Test lock-status endpoint.

acceptance_criteria.text: POST /api/priorities returns 409 when buy_lock active for country
DELETE /api/priorities/{id} returns 409 when buy_lock active
GET /api/priorities/lock-status returns correct lock state
Frontend disables all edit controls and shows lock banner during auto-buy

new_tasks.name: frontend-route-guards-all-pages
new_tasks.description: Add route-level 403 guards to all pages per IMP_02 section 8.3 navigation matrix. Currently only Admin has 403 guard.
new_tasks.instruction:
1. In each page component, wrap content with PermissionGate (or create a PageGuard wrapper).
2. Permission requirements per page:
   - Dashboard: purchase:read (all roles except none)
   - ITRP: purchase:read
   - ITRP/Settings: maintenance:read
   - ITRP/Maintenance: maintenance:read
   - ITRP/Priorities: purchase:read
   - Reporting/*: reporting:read
   - My Customers: purchase:read OR reporting:read
   - Manual Buy: purchase:execute
3. Fallback: Ant Design Result component with 403 status (same pattern as Admin page).
4. Test by logging in as opco user - should see 403 on Settings, Maintenance, Priorities, Manual Buy.

acceptance_criteria.text: Settings page shows 403 for Mid Office, OpCo roles
Maintenance page shows 403 for Mid Office, OpCo roles
Manual Buy page shows 403 for Mid Office, OpCo, GFI roles
All pages use consistent 403 fallback pattern

new_tasks.name: menu-visibility-per-role
new_tasks.description: Filter navigation menu items based on user role/permissions per IMP_02 section 8.3.
new_tasks.instruction:
In AppLayout.tsx:
1. Each menu item gets a requiredPermission field.
2. Use hasPermission() or hasAnyPermission() to filter menu items before rendering.
3. Menu visibility rules:
   - Dashboard: always visible
   - ITRP (+ sub-items): purchase:read
   - Settings: maintenance:read
   - Maintenance: maintenance:read
   - Priorities: purchase:read
   - Reporting: reporting:read
   - My Customers: purchase:read OR reporting:read
   - Manual Buy: purchase:execute
   - Admin: users:read OR audit:read (already done)
4. Remove XTRP menu item (dead link, page does not exist).
5. Test: OpCo user should only see Dashboard, Reporting, My Customers.

acceptance_criteria.text: OpCo user sees only Dashboard, Reporting, My Customers in menu
Mid Office user does not see Settings, Maintenance, Manual Buy, Admin
XTRP menu item removed

new_tasks.name: fix-my-customers-country-list
new_tasks.description: My Customers page uses hardcoded country list instead of allowedCountries() from auth store.
new_tasks.instruction:
In frontend/src/app/customers/page.tsx:
1. Replace hardcoded COUNTRIES array with allowedCountries() from useAuthStore.
2. If allowedCountries() returns null (global user), fetch country list from /api/buying/countries or /api/schedule/config.
3. Fallback: if no countries available, show empty state.
4. Ensure country dropdown only shows countries user has access to.

acceptance_criteria.text: Country dropdown in My Customers uses allowedCountries() not hardcoded list
OpCo user scoped to PL sees only PL in dropdown

new_tasks.name: back-office-country-scoping-seed
new_tasks.description: Update seed data to give Back Office user country scoping per D-017 decision.
new_tasks.instruction:
In backend/app/seed.py:
1. Change backoffice@randstad.com role assignment from scope_country=None to scope_country specific countries (e.g. PL, NL, DE - covering main test countries).
2. Or create multiple assignments: back_office+PL, back_office+NL.
3. Verify that after re-seed, backoffice user only sees assigned countries.
4. Update any integration tests that assume backoffice has global access.

acceptance_criteria.text: backoffice@randstad.com has country-scoped role assignments after seed
Back Office user only sees assigned countries in country dropdowns

new_tasks.name: log-unauthorized-access-attempts
new_tasks.description: Log denied access attempts to audit trail per REQ-018.
new_tasks.instruction:
In backend/app/auth/dependencies.py:
1. In require_permission(): when permission check fails (before raising 403), call log_audit with action=ACCESS_DENIED.
2. Add ACCESS_DENIED to ALLOWED_ACTIONS in audit/router.py.
3. Log: user_email, attempted resource:action, country (if applicable), timestamp.
4. In check_country_access(): when country access denied, also log_audit with ACCESS_DENIED.
5. Be careful: log_audit is async and needs db - may need to pass db through or use a simpler logging mechanism.

TEST:
6. Test that 403 response also creates audit entry.

acceptance_criteria.text: ACCESS_DENIED action type exists in ALLOWED_ACTIONS
Permission denial creates audit trail entry with user and attempted action
Country access denial creates audit trail entry

new_tasks.name: manual-buy-lock-check
new_tasks.description: Disable Manual Buy button when auto-buy is running for selected country.
new_tasks.instruction:
BACKEND:
1. Add GET /api/buying/lock-status?country=XX endpoint. Returns {locked: bool, locked_by: str, locked_at: timestamp}.

FRONTEND:
2. In manual-buy/page.tsx: before showing Run Auto-Buy button, check lock status. If locked, disable button and show warning.
3. In itrp/page.tsx: if queue button exists, check lock status for selected country.

TEST:
4. Test lock-status endpoint returns correct state.

acceptance_criteria.text: GET /api/buying/lock-status returns buy_lock state for country
Manual Buy button disabled with message when auto-buy lock active

new_tasks.name: firestore-schema-set-at-fix
new_tasks.description: Add missing set_at field to invoice_priorities schema in firestore_schema.py.
new_tasks.instruction:
In backend/app/firestore_schema.py, in INVOICE_PRIORITIES schema definition:
1. Add {name: set_at, type: timestamp, required: true, description: When priority was set} to fields array.
2. This is a docs-only fix - the field is already written by the router.
acceptance_criteria: set_at field present in INVOICE_PRIORITIES schema definition
